summary: Descriptive overview of recordingstatutes in substantive law: race, notice, race-notice, bona fide purchaser.
key_points: race is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for recordingstatutes are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine recordingstatutes in litigation.
The doctrine of recordingstatutes has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding recordingstatutes requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
